message:
sync beads: close caam-l19o.1.28 (TUI preferences config)

Completed TUI preferences integration:
- Wired TUI model to use LoadTUIPreferences() from SPM config
- Added structured debug logging via slog showing resolved TUI preferences

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"caam-l19o.1.25","title":"Task: Motion/reduced-motion unit tests","description":"Goal
- Validate spinner/progress behavior and reduced-motion handling.

Details
- Ensure spinners tick when motion is enabled and are static when reduced-motion or NO_COLOR is set.
- Verify progress bars render deterministically and do not overflow panel width.
- Emit structured test logs with motion mode, spinner type, and frame count.

Success
- Motion feels polished without breaking accessibility preferences.","notes":"Depends on: caam-l19o.1.6 (motion implementation) and caam-l19o.1.1 (theme system).","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-16T18:26:07.776867535Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-16T18:26:07.776867535Z","compaction_level":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"caam-l19o.1.25","depends_on_id":"caam-l19o.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-16T18:26:07.778737667Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","metadata":"","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"caam-l19o.1.25","depends_on_id":"caam-l19o.1.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-16T18:26:17.441394270Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","metadata":"","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"caam-l19o.1.25","depends_on_id":"caam-l19o.1.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-16T18:26:13.550839151Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","metadata":"","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"caam-l19o.1.26","title":"Task: Help render cache unit tests","description":"Goal
- Verify Glamour rendering is cached and does not re-render every frame.

Details
- Assert render is called once per content/theme change and reused otherwise.
- Validate cache invalidation when theme changes or help content updates.
- Emit structured test logs with cache hits/misses and render counts.

Success
- Help view performance is stable and predictable.","notes":"Depends on: caam-l19o.1.7 (help/onboarding).","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-16T18:26:22.857937663Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-16T18:26:22.857937663Z","compaction_level":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"caam-l19o.1.26","depends_on_id":"caam-l19o.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-16T18:26:22.859115471Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","metadata":"","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"caam-l19o.1.26","depends_on_id":"caam-l19o.1.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-16T18:26:26.781606475Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","metadata":"","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"caam-l19o.1.27","title":"Task: Focus/keymap unit tests","description":"Goal
- Validate focus cycling, panel activation, and keybinding consistency.

Details
- Test tab/shift+tab cycles focus in a predictable order.
- Ensure active panel indicators update with focus changes.
- Verify key hints match actual keybindings in each state.
- Emit structured test logs with focus index, active panel, and hint string.

Success
- Focus behavior is deterministic and discoverable.","notes":"Depends on: caam-l19o.1.8 (focus management) and caam-l19o.1.4 (status bar redesign).","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-16T18:26:35.011603729Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-16T18:26:35.011603729Z","compaction_level":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"caam-l19o.1.27","depends_on_id":"caam-l19o.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-16T18:26:35.012815792Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","metadata":"","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"caam-l19o.1.27","depends_on_id":"caam-l19o.1.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-16T18:26:46.060760359Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","metadata":"","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"caam-l19o.1.27","depends_on_id":"caam-l19o.1.8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-16T18:26:40.236952397Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","metadata":"","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"caam-l19o.1.28","title":"Task: TUI preferences config + docs","description":"Goal
- Centralize all TUI-specific preferences (theme, motion, toasts, mouse, key hints, density) in a single config surface with clear precedence and docs.

Background
- The Glamorous TUI work introduces multiple toggles (NO_COLOR, reduced motion, toasts, mouse, non-TTY fallback). Without a unified config, defaults and overrides are inconsistent and hard to discover.

Details
- Add a `tui` section in the main config file with documented defaults.
- Provide CLI flags and env overrides for the most important toggles:
  - theme: auto|dark|light
  - high-contrast: bool
  - reduced-motion: bool
  - toasts: bool
  - mouse: bool
  - density: cozy|compact
  - show-key-hints: bool
  - no-tui: bool (force non-TTY mode)
- Define precedence order: CLI flags > env vars > config file > defaults.
- Add a `caam config tui` or `caam tui --help` section that prints the effective config.
- Update README and TUI help to document toggles and examples.
- Add structured debug logs showing resolved TUI config (redacting anything sensitive).

Success
- Users can reliably tune the TUI without hunting for hidden flags, and defaults remain sensible.
","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-16T18:32:35.509696063Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-21T09:51:16.429177449Z","compaction_level":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"caam-l19o.1.28","depends_on_id":"caam-l19o.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-16T18:32:35.581840362Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","metadata":"","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"caam-l19o.1.29","title":"Task: TUI preferences config tests","description":"Goal
- Validate TUI config parsing, precedence rules, and toggle effects.

Details
- Unit-test precedence: CLI flags > env vars > config file > defaults.
- Verify each toggle changes behavior (theme selection, reduced motion disables spinners/toasts, no-tui fallback path, mouse enable/disable).
- Add fixtures for config file variations and missing fields.
- Emit structured test logs with resolved config values and source of each field.

Success
- Config behavior is deterministic and safe across environments.

 {"id":"caam-l19o.1.3","title":"Task: Modal overlays + focus rings","description":"Goal
- Render dialogs as true overlays with a dimmed background and clear focus indication.

Background
- Current dialog overlay discards the underlying view; there is no context or focus ring, which feels non-premium and disorienting.
- Skill guidance includes modal overlay patterns using lipgloss.Place/Overlay and double borders.

Details
- Implement an overlay that composes the dialog on top of the main view (retain background context).
- Add a subtle dim or desaturation layer to the background to emphasize focus.
- Use a premium border style for the active modal (double/thick) and a focused input cursor style.
- Clamp modal width/height to the viewport; support small-screen fallback.
- Add a keyboard hint line inside the modal with styled keycaps.

Tests
- Golden snapshots for modal overlay in compact + full layouts.
- Unit tests for size clamping and overlay positioning.
- E2E TUI test that opens dialogs and verifies background retention + focus ring.
- Structured test logs with modal dimensions and layout mode.

Success
- Dialogs feel modal, focused, and visually distinct without losing context.
","notes":"Depends on: caam-l19o.1.1 (theme system) and caam-ftpx (existing focus-indicator UX task).","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-16T18:11:30.554154643Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T10:04:10.764394626Z","closed_at":"2026-01-17T10:04:10.764394626Z","close_reason":"Completed","compaction_level":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"caam-l19o.1.3","depends_on_id":"caam-ftpx","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-16T18:11:37.459041520Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","metadata":"","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"caam-l19o.1.3","depends_on_id":"caam-l19o.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-16T18:11:30.555736562Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","metadata":"","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"caam-l19o.1.3","depends_on_id":"caam-l19o.1.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-16T18:11:34.189195020Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","metadata":"","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"caam-l19o.1.30","title":"Task: List + detail visual polish (rows, badges, hierarchy)","description":"Goal
- Make list rows and detail panels feel structured, scannable, and premium.

Background
- Current list rows are functional but visually flat; detail sections are dense and not clearly grouped.
- Glamorous TUI guidance emphasizes hierarchy, spacing, badges, and consistent row anatomy.

Details
- Standardize list row anatomy: left icon, primary label, secondary metadata, right-side status chip.
- Add subtle zebra striping or row separators; ensure selection + focus are clearly distinct.
- Use status badges (plan/status) with consistent colors and padding.
- Improve detail panel with labeled sections (Profile, Auth, Usage, Paths) using consistent spacing and divider rules.
- Right-align numeric values and keep units consistent (e.g., minutes, days, GB).
- Apply truncation rules with ellipsis for long names; show full value on focus where possible.

Tests
- Add golden snapshots for list rows + detail sections and truncation behavior (see caam-l19o.1.32).
- Emit structured test logs with row width, truncation decisions, and badge styles.

Success
- Lists are easy to scan, and details read like a structured card rather than a wall of text.
","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-16T18:33:28.040372461Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-16T21:55:48.855124261Z","compaction_level":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"caam-l19o.1.30","depends_on_id":"caam-l19o.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-16T18:33:28.093116193Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","metadata":"","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"caam-l19o.1.30","depends_on_id":"caam-l19o.1.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-16T18:34:24.676409938Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","metadata":"","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"caam-l19o.1.30","depends_on_id":"caam-l19o.1.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-16T18:34:28.460760142Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","metadata":"","thread_id":""}]}

Rename sidecar files alongside the corrupt db and add a regression test.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"","created_at":"2026-01-13T02:54:54.676064452Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-13T02:57:02.994029306Z","closed_at":"2026-01-13T02:57:02.994029306Z","close_reason":"Rename WAL/SHM sidecars when moving corrupt db; added renameSQLiteSidecars test","compaction_level":0}
 {"id":"caam-ldjp","title":"Create enhanced test harness with detailed logging","description":"# Create Enhanced Test Harness

## Goal
Extend testutil.Harness with comprehensive structured logging for all e2e tests.

## Current Harness Location
internal/testutil/harness.go

## Required Enhancements

### Structured Logging
```go
type ExtendedHarness struct {
    *Harness
    logBuffer   *bytes.Buffer
    stepLogs    []StepLog
    metrics     map[string]time.Duration
}

type StepLog struct {
    Name      string
    Timestamp time.Time
    Duration  time.Duration
    Level     LogLevel
    Message   string
    Data      map[string]interface{}
}
```

### Required Methods
```go
func (h *ExtendedHarness) StartStep(name, description string)
func (h *ExtendedHarness) EndStep(name string)
func (h *ExtendedHarness) LogInfo(msg string, data ...interface{})
func (h *ExtendedHarness) LogDebug(msg string, data ...interface{})
func (h *ExtendedHarness) LogError(err error, context string)
func (h *ExtendedHarness) RecordMetric(name string, value time.Duration)
func (h *ExtendedHarness) DumpLogs() string
func (h *ExtendedHarness) Summary() string
```

### Features
1. Timestamp each log entry
2. Track duration of each step
3. JSON-formatted structured data
4. Automatic cleanup logging
5. Test summary with metrics

## File: internal/testutil/extended_harness.go

## Acceptance Criteria
- Backwards compatible with existing Harness
- Clear, readable log output
- Supports nested steps
- Metrics collection for performance tests","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"","created_at":"2025-12-20T00:06:06.776731881Z","updated_at":"2025-12-20T01:34:11.179742332Z","closed_at":"2025-12-20T01:34:11.179742332Z","close_reason":"Implemented ExtendedHarness with step tracking, logging, metrics, and summary methods. Fixed mutex deadlock. All tests pass.","compaction_level":0}
 {"id":"caam-ldxo","title":"Epic: Installation, Updates, and Dependency Bootstrap","description":"Purpose
- Make caam installs, updates, and dependency management bulletproof, idempotent, and user-friendly across macOS/Linux, including multi-machine setups.

Background
- Current install.sh fetches GitHub releases but does not verify checksums or signatures, has limited cache-busting, and lacks a built-in self-update command.
- Local repos (repo_updater, xf) already demonstrate stronger patterns: checksum verification, cache-busting, optional gum UI, and interactive confirmations.
- Recent tooling ecosystem trends emphasize signed release artifacts and explicit verification (e.g., cosign-verified checksums in Charm tools).

Scope
- Harden install.sh with checksum/signature verification, cache-busting, and safe fallbacks.
- Add caam update/self-update workflows with atomic replace and rollback.
- Provide dependency bootstrap (gum, wezterm, tailscale, playwright, gh, jq) with safe prompts and non-interactive mode.
- Support auto-update scheduling (opt-in) and distributed update orchestration.
- Add comprehensive installer/update tests and logging.

Non-Goals
- Shipping a package manager; use system package managers when possible.

Success Criteria
- Installs verify integrity, upgrades are idempotent, and missing dependencies are handled gracefully.
- Updates and dependency bootstrap are auditable, reversible, and do not surprise users.
","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","assignee":"","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-16T18:50:00.356724426Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-16T18:50:00.356724426Z","compaction_level":0}
-{"id":"caam-ldxo.1","title":"Task: Release artifacts signing + checksum verification","description":"Goal
- Ensure every release includes machine-verifiable integrity (checksums + signatures) and the installer consumes those artifacts.

Background
- caam install.sh currently does not verify checksums or signatures.
- Charm tools now ship signed checksums (cosign), which is a good integrity baseline.

Details
- Update release pipeline (goreleaser) to publish SHA256SUMS + SHA256SUMS.sig (cosign).
- Decide on key distribution (cosign public key embedded in repo OR keyless GitHub OIDC with Rekor log verification).
- Include an explicit release manifest (version, build date, git SHA, go version) for verification + debugging.
- Document verification commands for users (cosign verify-blob + sha256sum).
- Keep signing optional for local snapshots, required for tagged releases.

Tests
- CI job that fails if release artifacts lack checksum + signature.
- Test harness that validates checksum files against a staged artifact and verifies signature.

Success
- Every tagged release includes checksums and signatures, and installer/update flows verify them before install.
","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-16T18:50:07.650248513Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-16T21:53:00.948572301Z","compaction_level":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"caam-ldxo.1","depends_on_id":"caam-ldxo","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-16T18:50:07.651430109Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","metadata":"","thread_id":""}]}
+{"id":"caam-ldxo.1","title":"Task: Release artifacts signing + checksum verification","description":"Goal
- Ensure every release includes machine-verifiable integrity (checksums + signatures) and the installer consumes those artifacts.

Background
- caam install.sh currently does not verify checksums or signatures.
- Charm tools now ship signed checksums (cosign), which is a good integrity baseline.

Details
- Update release pipeline (goreleaser) to publish SHA256SUMS + SHA256SUMS.sig (cosign).
- Decide on key distribution (cosign public key embedded in repo OR keyless GitHub OIDC with Rekor log verification).
- Include an explicit release manifest (version, build date, git SHA, go version) for verification + debugging.
- Document verification commands for users (cosign verify-blob + sha256sum).
- Keep signing optional for local snapshots, required for tagged releases.

Tests
- CI job that fails if release artifacts lack checksum + signature.
- Test harness that validates checksum files against a staged artifact and verifies signature.

Success
- Every tagged release includes checksums and signatures, and installer/update flows verify them before install.
","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-16T18:50:07.650248513Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-21T17:07:12.524210352Z","compaction_level":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"caam-ldxo.1","depends_on_id":"caam-ldxo","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-16T18:50:07.651430109Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","metadata":"","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"caam-ldxo.2","title":"Task: Harden install.sh (integrity + gum UX + cache-busting)","description":"Goal
- Make the installer safer, clearer, and more reliable while remaining a copy/paste one-liner.

Background
- Local installers (repo_updater, xf) show best practices: cache-busting, gum-enhanced output, non-interactive mode, and checksum verification.
- We need parity for caam with explicit integrity checks and predictable logging.

Details
- Add checksum verification + signature validation (from caam-ldxo.1).
- Add cache-busting query param in URLs (opt-out env var).
- Add gum-based formatting if gum is installed and stdout is a TTY; graceful fallback otherwise.
- Add NO_COLOR + CI/non-interactive behavior (no prompts, deterministic exit codes).
- Add explicit --verify option (run caam --version and optional self-test).
- Add explicit --channel=stable|beta and --version override.
- Ensure install is idempotent: if the same version is installed, skip download unless --force.
- Preserve previous binary as backup and restore if replace fails.
- Improve error messages with actionable guidance (missing curl/wget, PATH, permissions).

Tests
- Shell tests for: checksum failure, signature failure, gum present/absent, non-tty, cache-bust on/off, version already installed, rollback on partial failure.
- Mock GitHub API responses to validate parsing and fallback behavior.

Success
- Installer is safe, audit-friendly, and pleasant to read in the terminal.
","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-16T18:50:18.723957303Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-16T21:53:09.289837881Z","compaction_level":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"caam-ldxo.2","depends_on_id":"caam-ldxo","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-16T18:50:18.725403277Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","metadata":"","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"caam-ldxo.2","depends_on_id":"caam-ldxo.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-16T18:51:19.701293431Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","metadata":"","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"caam-ldxo.3","title":"Task: Self-update command (caam update)","description":"Goal
- Provide an in-product, idempotent update path that verifies integrity and supports rollback.

Details
- Add `caam update` (and `caam update --check`) to fetch latest release metadata.
- Verify SHA256 + signature before replacing binary.
- Use atomic replace with temp file + rename; keep last-known-good binary for rollback.
- Support channels (stable/beta) and explicit version pinning.
- Emit structured logs (version_from, version_to, download_url, checksum_ok, signature_ok).
- Honor non-interactive mode (CI) with clear exit codes for "up-to-date", "updated", "failed".

Tests
- Unit tests for release metadata parsing and version comparison.
- Integration test with a fake local release server and bad checksum/signature cases.
- E2E test that verifies rollback restores previous version on failure.

Success
- Users can upgrade safely without re-running curl | bash.
","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-16T18:50:25.027082534Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-16T21:53:15.563573020Z","compaction_level":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"caam-ldxo.3","depends_on_id":"caam-ldxo","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-16T18:50:25.028567021Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","metadata":"","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"caam-ldxo.3","depends_on_id":"caam-ldxo.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-16T18:51:31.725586041Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","metadata":"","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"caam-ldxo.4","title":"Task: Auto-update scheduler + distributed fleet upgrade","description":"Goal
- Offer opt-in background update checks and coordinated upgrades across remote coordinators.

Details
- Add a scheduler (daemon/cron helper) to run `caam update --check` daily and surface notifications in TUI/CLI.
- For distributed auth recovery, provide `caam update --remotes` to update coordinator binaries via SSH/SFTP with checksum verification.
- Maintain idempotency: skip if versions match, and provide a --dry-run report.
- Add a rollback command to revert remote nodes to last-known-good.
- Ensure update checks are opt-in by default and never auto-apply without explicit consent.

Tests
- Dry-run snapshot tests for local + remote update plans.
- Mock SSH/SFTP tests to validate version detection, upgrade ordering, and rollback.
- Log validation tests to ensure structured logs include node, version, and outcome.

Success
- Users can keep single or multi-machine setups current with minimal friction.
